events.title: Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world
events.description: Environment Canada has issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellow Smoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the current worst in the world and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the north-east US. Environment Canada issued health warnings on Wednesday after the sky over country’s largest city turned a sickly yellow and was ranked the worst in the world according to IQAir , the Swiss technology company that racks global air quality. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world

events.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
events.description: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents say Nigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official. Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a statement. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara

events.title: ‘God is punishing the politicians’: anger at earthquake response grows in Venezuela
events.description: Threat of social unrest rises as public indignation at lack of disaster aid comes on top of fallout from US military intervention • A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela Public anger at what many perceive as the Venezuelan government’s botched response to twin earthquakes that killed nearly 4,500 people is growing, with one grieving mother caught on camera berating the son of former president Nicolás Maduro. Maduro’s politician son received a hostile reception while visiting a semi-destroyed social housing project named after his father’s late mentor Hugo Chávez. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘God is punishing the politicians’: anger at earthquake response grows in Venezuela

events.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
events.description: Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial. It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists said, with patients enrolled just six weeks after the outbreak being declared a public health emergency of international concern by the World Health Organization (WHO) on 17 May. Continue reading...

events.title: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
events.description: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say

events.title: Bangkok bar fire: death toll reaches 30 as police say negligence is ‘primary theory’
events.description: Bar owner offers ‘deepest apologies’ as police investigate whether exits were either blocked or hard to access The Bangkok pub that became the scene of the city’s deadliest blaze in 17 years has said it will cooperate with an investigation into alleged negligence, as the death toll rose to 30. The local district office said on Tuesday that three more people had died after the devastating fire that broke out in the early hours of Monday. An initial assessment by disaster officials found an electrical short ‌circuit in an air conditioner located in the ‌ceiling had caused the fire. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bangkok bar fire: death toll reaches 30 as police say negligence is ‘primary theory’

events.title: Gunfire shatters Toronto Latin street festival, leaving at least 2 dead and multiple wounded
events.description: At least two people were killed and four others wounded after gunfire erupted at Toronto's TD Salsa on St. Clair Festival. Police are investigating the deadly shooting.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Gunfire shatters Toronto Latin street festival, leaving at least 2 dead and multiple wounded

events.title: Confirmed Ebola cases top 2,000 in Congo, including 754 deaths
events.description: Ebola cases in Congo have reached 2,011, with 754 deaths, according to government data

events.title: ‘Cycle of escalation must end’: UN condemns deadly Strait of Hormuz attacks
events.description: The UN maritime agency, IMO, condemned overnight attacks on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz that killed at least two seafarers as fresh strikes were reported early Tuesday in the escalating US-Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Cycle of escalation must end’: UN condemns deadly Strait of Hormuz attacks

events.title: Killings continue on Del Monte farm in Kenya, families say, after G4S hired for security
events.description: Exclusive: Three men killed in incidents over past year allegedly involving G4S guards, who replaced in-house team after previous deaths Bereaved families and politicians have raised alarm about continued killings on Del Monte’s pineapple farm in Kenya despite the company hiring a British security firm to replace its in-house security team after previous deaths were exposed by the Guardian. The multinational food company appointed G4S to guard the farm, which is estimated to cover at least 40 sq km, the area of a small city, after the Guardian detailed allegations of brutal assaults and killings of people suspected of trespassing on its land. Kenyan police have been working with G4S to guard the site. Continue reading...

events.title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to war
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to war

events.title: Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur
events.description: The Security Council meets at 3pm on Wednesday for an update on the International Criminal Court's work in Sudan's Darfur region, referred to the Court back in 2005. Focus has recently turned to atrocities in the ongoing war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The briefing comes as the ICC faces mounting pressure: this week, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio launched a campaign to “dismantle” the Court, which prosecutes individuals for genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression. Follow full live coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur

events.title: ‘This is a fire’: DRC Ebola outbreak is fastest-growing ever, warns WHO
events.description: Infections of the Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have reached record highs and a majority of new cases are coming from “unknown chains of transmission”, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘This is a fire’: DRC Ebola outbreak is fastest-growing ever, warns WHO

events.title: Venezuela’s interim government and opposition to begin formal talks
events.description: Nobel laureate María Corina Machado will not lead negotiations over new elections, contrary to expectations The interim government of Venezuela has announced it will begin formal talks with the opposition aimed at “strengthening democracy” in the country. The move is backed by the US, which says it is seeking a “democratic transition” in a country still recovering from the twin earthquakes that killed more than 4,700 people. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela’s interim government and opposition to begin formal talks

events.title: United by grief, mothers in Brazil demand reparations after police killed their sons

events.title: US launches ‘wave of strikes’ on Iran, Tehran threatens to halt regional energy exports

Tehran responded with strikes on “US military targets” in Gulf states and threatened to halt energy exports in the region.

events.title: ‘Hunted’ by drones: UN reports worst month for Ukraine’s civilians since 2022
events.description: Civilian casualties in Ukraine soared during the first half of 2026 amid escalating Russian attacks and intensifying use of deadly weapons, the UN human rights monitoring mission in the country, HRMMU, said on Tuesday.

events.title: International Criminal Court ‘a critical cog’ in global justice efforts

events.title: China’s monthly car ‌exports top 1m for first time as overall trade soars
events.description: Country risks new tariffs from US and EU as it looks likely to match or beat last year’s record surplus of $1tn Business live – latest updates China’s monthly car ‌exports topped 1m for the first time in June as overall overseas shipments from the world’s second biggest economy rose 27%. Official Chinese customs data showed that a stronger-than-expected trade performance kept China on track to match or beat last year’s record trade surplus of $1tn (£748bn), achieved despite Donald Trump’s curtailed tariff war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China’s monthly car ‌exports top 1m for first time as overall trade soars

events.title: People continue to stand by refugees, Gen Z shows most empathy
events.description: Protection for refugees has been enshrined in international law for more than seven decades, but how much support is there today for those fleeing conflict and persecution?

events.title: Derision at US chain Chipotle’s plan to sell its Mexican food in Mexico
events.description: One social media user described the opening of its first outlet in Monterrey as ‘like the dog teaching a duck to fly’ A US chain’s plan to sell its version of Mexican food to Mexicans with a first branch south of the border has prompted bemusement, skepticism and anger among local people. Chipotle Mexican Grill, known for its customisable burritos, tacos and bowls, has more than 4,000 locations worldwide and announced on Tuesday that it was expanding into Mexico in what it described as a significant milestone. Continue reading...

events.title: Almost 145,000 Australians will lose support for autism under NDIS reforms, documents reveal
events.description: Internal estimates show 144,600 people will be taken off the scheme by 2030, many of them under 18 Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Almost 145,000 Australians receiving support for autism on the national disability insurance scheme (NDIS) are expected to be removed by the end of the decade, internal estimates show, as the federal government moves ahead with plans to narrow the focus of the “diluted” $52bn scheme toward those with significant and complex needs. From 2028, almost two-thirds of the 241,000 participants set to lose access to the NDIS will be aged 18 or under, health department documents released to Guardian Australia under freedom of information laws show. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Almost 145,000 Australians will lose support for autism under NDIS reforms, documents reveal

events.title: Ukraine: Kyiv and EU agree on new drone production deal

events.title: Following years of wildfires, Europe must 'diversify' landscapes and strengthen land management
events.description: For Spotlight, François Picard is pleased to welcome Johan Sjöström, Senior Research Scientist at RISE Research Institutes of Sweden. He argues that the devastating wildfires affecting northern France are not an isolated anomaly but part of a "new normal" for Europe. His focus moves beyond extreme weather to examine the interaction between climate, vegetation, land management and institutional preparedness.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Following years of wildfires, Europe must 'diversify' landscapes and strengthen land management

events.title: ICE ordered to suspend most vehicle stops after second deadly shooting
events.description: Trump administration officials told Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers to suspend most vehicle stops after two deadly shootings within a week, people familiar with the decision said Tuesday. The policy change came after an ICE officer shot and killed a Colombian driver Monday in Maine and a week after one shot and killed a motorist in Houston.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 ICE ordered to suspend most vehicle stops after second deadly shooting

events.title: Trump says ICE should continue traffic stops, contradicting earlier suspension
events.description: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) should continue to use traffic stops despite a decision the previous day to suspend the practice. The ban on vehicle stops came after an ICE agent fatally shot a Colombian driver in Maine and another motorist was killed in Texas last week by immigration officers.

events.title: Family of girl injured in mass-stabbing welcome baby boy

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside

events.title: Why the US is launching naval drones at Iran’s port city of Bandar Abbas

events.title: Developing countries spend more repaying foreign debt than on education, UN reveals
events.description: Unesco report shows children lost out to servicing debt in 113 countries, with 18 spending five times more on loans Most developing countries spent less on education than they did repaying debt last year, according to the UN, at the same time as global aid to education is predicted to decline by up to 30%. More was spent on servicing foreign debt than on education in 113 developing countries in 2025, according to research by the UN’s culture and education agency, Unesco. In sub-Saharan Africa, countries spent 3.6 times more on debt than education . Continue reading...

events.title: No support for Jews fleeing Nazi Germany at Evian conference
events.description: In July 1938, a conference was held in Evian, France, to address the issue of Jews being persecuted by the Nazis in Germany and Austria. Almost nobody in the world was willing to take them in as refugees.

events.title: Running dry: How to store more groundwater for dry seasons

events.title: Does Marco Rubio control Venezuela?

A report from the New York Times suggests that the US Secretary of State now effectively controls the Latin American country's finances, the distribution of its natural resources and its government since the capture of Nicolas Maduro. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis speaks with Christopher Sabatini, Director of Chatham House’s Latin America program.

events.description: Old rivals. New stakes. A World Cup final spot on the line. Argentina vs. England.

events.title: England vs. Argentina: A football rivalry full of history
events.description: From the Falkland Islands to Diego Maradona and David Beckham, there is so much history between the two 2026 World Cup semifinalists.

events.title: England-Argentina live: Kane and Messi battle for World Cup final spot
events.description: England and Argentina clash in an eagerly awaited World Cup semi-final in Atlanta, with both nations seeking the last stage. The Three Lions and the Albiceleste bring history, rivalry, and star quality to this decisive encounter, with Harry Kane's England facing Lionel Messi's Argentina in a battle for the ages. Follow our liveblog for minute-by-minute commentary.

events.title: The Odyssey: Matt Damon, Lupita Nyong’o, Anne Hathaway relive the epic
events.description: In this edition, Critic Emma Jones tells us why Christopher Nolan’s take on the Greek epic was worth the wait – and the eye-watering budget – and how the themes of this ancient story still speak to contemporary audiences. The all-star cast includes Matt Damon as a world-weary Odysseus, Anne Hathaway embodies a very patient Penelope and Lupita Nyong’O plays both Helen of Troy and her twin sister Clytemnestra.

events.title: Briton jailed in Iran given additional two-year sentence, family says
events.description: The family ⁠of British ​couple Craig and Lindsay Foreman, who are serving a 10-year ​prison sentence in Iran on espionage charges that they deny, said on Wednesday that a judge had extended Craig Foreman's ​sentence ‌by two years for speaking to the ⁠media. The couple were arrested in 2025 while travelling through Iran by motorcycle ‌as part of a round-the-world journey. Britain has described ⁠their original sentences as "totally unjustifiable".

events.title: World Cup football’s other goal: Helping young people talk about mental health
events.description: When Dr. Sahira Al Nahari founded Shifā Art, a Saudi Arabia-based organization using art to create conversations around mental health, she noticed that men attending her therapeutic art workshops often felt uncomfortable opening up about their feelings.

events.title: World News in Brief: Gaza aid challenges persist, renewed push for clean energy, Sudan cholera update
events.description: Humanitarians in the Gaza Strip continue to face significant challenges in reaching communities located near the so-called “Yellow Line” in northern Rafah, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Tuesday.

events.title: Despite hurdles, don’t give up on Sustainable Development Goals, UN urges
events.description: In a world of “parallel realities” where stark inequalities seemingly divide people and challenge the promise of multilateralism, the vision of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) is to bring people together and help them achieve a better reality.

events.title: Three teen girls arrested in New Jersey for allegedly throwing acid on six women in drive-by attack
events.description: Three teen girls on a moped allegedly threw sulfuric acid at six women in Jersey City, causing skin burns and sending one victim to a burn unit.

events.title: Rastafarians in Kenya fail in bid to get cannabis legalised for religious purposes
events.description: The court acknowledged the need for a broader national debate on cannabis policy in Kenya.

events.title: Palestinian homes sealed off by Israeli military restrictions
events.description: An Israeli military order imposed after a series of settler attacks has left families cut off from parts of their home.

events.title: Prominent Moroccan journalist Ali Lmrabet released from custody
events.description: Moroccan activists warn of an intensifying crackdown on regime critics after the arrest of a journalist and a rapper.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Prominent Moroccan journalist Ali Lmrabet released from custody

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
events.description: Aid worker flown to Berlin as Trump administration bars Americans from traveling to US on commercial flights A US national who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another American infected with Ebola in the DRC was treated in Berlin. Meanwhile, the Trump administration on Monday said it was blocking American citizens in ⁠the DRC from traveling to the US on commercial flights, Reuters reported, citing a White House official. Continue reading...

events.title: Patient records stolen in cyber-attack on Australian healthcare provider
events.description: Partnered Health says personal information and medical details and notes were breached in the attack on 21 clinics Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Thousands of medical records and patient information stored with one of Australia’s biggest healthcare providers have been breached in a cyber-attack. Partnered Health, owned by private equity firm Quadrant, said 21 clinics across several cities including Sydney, Melbourne and Canberra have been affected by the breach which occurred on 23 June. Continue reading...

events.title: Acting AG Todd Blanche faces a key test. And, ICE pauses most traffic stops
events.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is set to appear before the Senate for his confirmation hearing. And, Immigration and Customs Enforcement has paused most traffic stops after recent deadly shootings.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Acting AG Todd Blanche faces a key test. And, ICE pauses most traffic stops

events.title: 'You cannot have a good assisted dying law without good palliative care'
events.description: For Spotlight, François Picard is pleased to welcome Anne Reynaud, Board Member of the Association for the Right to Die with Dignity. France's long awaited assisted dying legislation marks a profound shift in the country's approach to end of life care, but as Reynaud makes clear, the parliamentary vote is less the end of a debate than the beginning of a new ethical and legal era. Speaking from both personal experience and her professional role, Reynaud argues that the law represents meaningful progress while remaining more restrictive than the recommendations of France's Citizens' Convention.

events.title: Count Binface: The joke candidate that could enter the British Parliament
events.description: Could an intergalactic space warrior become the #UK's newest member of #parliament? Now that he is the only likely challenger to Nigel #Farage's seat in the #Clacton-on-Sea by-election, the chances of him entering the House of Commons have never been this high.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Count Binface: The joke candidate that could enter the British Parliament

events.title: Europe: National elections may bring "very different Coalition of the Willing next year"
events.description: 🇺🇦 "There could be very different representation at the coalition of the willing next year." Kira Rudik assesses how political support for #Ukraine may shift with the results of key upcoming #European national elections. More on #F24Debate➡️https://f24.my/C3LG

events.title: Why is Trump risking a 'forever war' with Iran?
events.description: 👉Listen to This Is Why on your podcast app👈
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why is Trump risking a 'forever war' with Iran?

events.title: Lebanon and Israel move toward implementing withdrawal agreement, US officials say
events.description: Lebanon and Israel have taken steps toward creating “pilot zones” in southern Lebanon, following U.S.-mediated talks in Rome
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon and Israel move toward implementing withdrawal agreement, US officials say

events.title: Colombia: Pockets of insecurity challenge ‘vibrant democracy’, Security Council told
events.description: As Colombia awaits the inauguration of its newly elected president, pockets of insecurity remain a challenge for a nation that has overcome decades of conflict, the head of the UN verification team there told the Security Council on Wednesday.

events.title: Looted gold and gum arabic are bankrolling Sudan's war, UN warns
events.description: Alongside gold, the little-known Sudanese commodity gum arabic – used in soft drinks, food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals – continues to fuel the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Looted gold and gum arabic are bankrolling Sudan's war, UN warns

events.title: Man accused of attacking, attempting to drown female jogger along Houston bayou: police
events.description: Deputies used a drone above White Oak Bayou and followed bike tracks to locate the suspect after the woman fought back and escaped the attack.

events.title: Alabama sheriff details gruesome murders of veteran and wife inside their own home
events.description: George Birmingham allegedly killed a military veteran and his wife with an electric saw and metallic object during a burglary in Reform, Alabama.

events.title: Rescuers work to save nearly 50 bottlenose dolphins in historic Cape Cod stranding
events.description: Nearly 50 bottlenose dolphins stranded across Cape Cod in what IFAW calls the largest known dolphin stranding in Massachusetts history so far.

events.title: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay ministers $356,000 in defamation case
events.description: Bloomberg’s editor-in-chief, John Micklethwait, stands by reporting, saying ministers who sued ‘imposed an extremely strained meaning on what was a solid ​story’ Bloomberg News and one of its reporters have been ordered to pay S$460,000 (US$355,734) in damages after an article it published was found to have defamed two Singapore government ministers, the city-state’s high court said in a judgment released on Tuesday. Bloomberg and the reporter, Low De Wei, are liable to jointly pay S$230,000 to each minister, comprising S$170,000 in general damages and S$60,000 in aggravated damages, ⁠the judgment said. Continue reading...

events.title: Irish police under fire for refusing to identify reported asylum seeker sought in American mother's murder
events.description: Irish police refuse to name the person of interest in Jamey Carney's Killarney murder. The suspect fled to Turkey before her body was found.

events.title: Iran unveils huge billboard showing Trump in coffin
events.description: Iran has installed a billboard in the capital Tehran showing US President Donald Trump lying in a coffin.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Albanese’s AI plan is admirable – but will face tech giants more powerful than most national governments
events.description: Challenges of regulating social media or stopping hate speech show these firms can set their own terms and prices for countries like Australia Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese took a trip back in time during his much anticipated speech on artificial intelligence on Wednesday. Seeking to harness the momentous change bearing down on our lives, the prime minister told an audience at the University of Sydney that his government would keep pace with AI, even seeking to “get out in front” of the technological tidal wave. Continue reading...

events.title: France’s National Assembly will give final approval to assisted-dying bill
events.description: France's National Assembly is set to give final approval to a bill allowing adults with incurable illnesses to receive lethal medication

events.title: Heatwave prompts Italy's delivery riders to strike for extra rights
events.description: Delivery riders in Milan, Bologna and Florence want to protect their health and their wages.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Heatwave prompts Italy's delivery riders to strike for extra rights

events.title: Boss of fake government agency arrested in Nigeria after weeks on the run
events.description: The case has gripped Nigeria since President Bola Tinubu ordered a corruption investigation last week.

events.title: Gas tanker explodes in fire near army barracks in Lebanon
events.description: A gas tanker exploded after soaring temperatures sparked a fire near the Lebanese Army barracks in Hermel.

events.title: China’s economy grows at 4.3%, one of its lowest rates on record
events.description: Worse-than-expected figures for three months to June come amid concerns over lopsided economy Business live – latest updates China has posted worse-than-expected growth figures for the three months to June as its economy expanded by just 4.3% – one of its lowest quarterly readings on record. The rate, which came in under the government’s target of 4.5% to 5%, was one of the weakest since reporting on official quarterly GDP figures began in the early 1990s. Continue reading...

events.title: The U.S. and Iran standoff over the Strait of Hormuz intensifies
events.description: Iran threatened to block all oil exporting routes in the region on Wednesday in response to the U.S. maritime blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Palestinians in Gaza: Stuck between Hamas and Israel
events.description: Hamas is planning to transfer control of Gaza's civilian administration to Palestinian technocrats as part of the fragile US ceasefire. How is that affecting the daily lives of people living in Gaza?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Palestinians in Gaza: Stuck between Hamas and Israel

events.title: US senators demand findings of probe into deadly strike on Iran school
events.description: Senators have demanded the US military urgently reveals the findings of its probe into a deadly missile strike on an Iranian school.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US senators demand findings of probe into deadly strike on Iran school

events.title: Police arrest man who threatened to shoot Reform UK party leader Nigel Farage
events.description: London police have arrested a man who threatened to shoot Reform UK party leader Nigel Farage on social media

events.title: Spanish authorities identify all 13 victims of deadly southern fire
events.description: Spanish authorities have identified all 13 victims of a devastating fire in a remote expat community in southern Spain
events.ticker_label: 💥 Spanish authorities identify all 13 victims of deadly southern fire

events.title: California suspect's alleged $30K meth stash seized after he left backpack in rideshare: police
events.description: A Sacramento man was arrested after police said he accidentally left a backpack containing 355.7 grams of suspected methamphetamine in a vehicle he was riding in.

events.title: Mike Huckabee rejects Ro Khanna's Israel visit claims: 'Not held at gunpoint'
events.description: Ro Khanna claims violent Israeli settlers detained his delegation for 75 minutes in the West Bank, and U.S. Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee angrily fired back.

events.title: Massive fire rips through pub, killing at least 27 as patrons flee smoke-filled venue
events.description: Officials said the fire broke out around midnight at a northern Bangkok pub, sending thick smoke through the venue as patrons tried to escape.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Massive fire rips through pub, killing at least 27 as patrons flee smoke-filled venue

events.title: Migrant sought in American mom's killing was denied asylum but remained in Ireland
events.description: A migrant whose asylum claim was rejected fled Ireland to Istanbul before the murder investigation began, sparking an Interpol and Europol manhunt.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Migrant sought in American mom's killing was denied asylum but remained in Ireland

events.title: New Germany sex-crime figures reignite migration fight as exploitation probe expands

events.title: Zelenskyy backs state energy company chief Koretskyi for new Ukraine PM
events.description: Ukraine's parliament is expected to vote for the next prime minister on Thursday.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Japan officials hunt bear that raided couple’s fridge amid string of break-ins
events.description: Officials set up traps and electric fences after 14 break-ins recored in one town in two weeks, amid fears of a repeat offender Authorities are searching for a bear that sneaked into the home of an elderly couple and raided their fridge amid concerns it may be behind 14 break-ins across a Japanese town in the past fortnight. On Monday evening, Mitsuo Matsubara, 87, was confronted by a large Asiatic black bear when he went to investigate a noise in his kitchen. His fridge was open , and food was strewn across the floor. His wife called the police. Continue reading...

events.title: Meet the millionaires investing in nature conservation
events.description: In France, a handful of wealthy entrepreneurs are buying up hundreds of hectares of land with the goal of letting nature reclaim its rightful place. Among them is Rodolphe Landemaine, an artisan baker at the helm of 30 bakeries in France and Japan. He routinely opens the doors to his Normandy estate to other business leaders in hopes that they, too, will put their money to good use.

events.title: Zambia's Guy Scott, who was Africa's first white head of state in decades, has died at 82
events.description: Guy Scott, the Zambian politician who briefly served as acting president to become Africa’s first white head of state in more than two decades, has died
events.ticker_label: 💥 Zambia's Guy Scott, who was Africa's first white head of state in decades, has died at 82

events.title: Hong Kong booksellers are reportedly arrested over alleged sales of seditious publications
events.description: Local media are reporting that authorities in Hong Kong have raided two bookstores and arrested five people for allegedly selling seditious publications

events.title: Dem lawmaker says Constitution barred police from stopping her after alleged 100 mph speeding
events.description: State Rep. Ellen Read claims a 1784 New Hampshire Constitution provision shields her from arrest while traveling to or from legislative session.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: 150 years of the Bayreuth Festival: Confronting a troubled legacy
events.description: Should the past be confronted — or avoided? Why the Wagner festival's handling of its Nazi-era legacy remains a contentious issue in its 150th anniversary year.

events.title: Assisted dying bill 'almost anathema to France's right wing'
events.description: French lawmakers will vote on Wednesday on a bill that ​would create a legal right to assisted dying for adults with incurable illnesses, capping an intense ethical and political debate. But the bill is highly controversial and has faced opposition in particular from France's right, explains France 24's Luke Shrago.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Gibraltar and Spain end border checks in boost for businesses
events.description: Thousands of people who travel every day between the southern tip of Spain and the British territory of Gibraltar will no longer have to cross a physical border, beginning on Wednesday. The official opening at midnight on Tuesday, after a border fence was removed, allows a new freedom of movement under a historic treaty between the European Union and the United Kingdom and will come as a welcome boost for local businesses.

events.title: Horde of golden mini-Mozarts marks 270 years since the composer's birth in Salzburg
events.description: Visitors to Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart’s hometown can see the composer — and his dog — in miniature as Salzburg celebrates the 270th anniversary of his birth

events.title: Face-to-face with ancient Rome: Exhibition in Hungary brings forgotten faces to life
events.description: A new exhibition in Hungary brings ancient residents of the Roman frontier city of Aquincum to life with detailed facial reconstructions

events.title: Family stopped alleged child kidnapper as police say staffing shortage delayed response up to 40 minutes
events.description: Portland police had just 41 patrol officers citywide when a naked man allegedly tried to kidnap a 7-year-old tourist at Tom McCall Waterfront Park.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Family stopped alleged child kidnapper as police say staffing shortage delayed response up to 40 minutes

events.title: French MPs approve assisted dying law with strict rules after years of argument
events.description: The bill would allow assisted dying for terminally ill adults who meet strict criteria.

events.title: Five arrested after Hong Kong police raid independent bookshops
events.description: Officials say they are suspected of selling "seditious" books which incited "hatred" against authorities.

events.description: The defeat triggered opposition calls for Meloni to resign ahead of next year's general election.

events.description: The opposition says the aim of the talks is to strengthen the democratic institutions and the electoral system.

events.title: Sierra Leone drops treason charge against ex-president over attempted coup
events.description: Ernest Bai Koroma was arrested in 2023 after gunmen broke into an armoury and some jails, freeing inmates.

events.description: The president had sought to delay the payment as he tried to persuade the Supreme Court to overturn the judgement.

events.description: Production will stop for two years at the mine which employs more than 4,000 people.

events.description: Several recent elections have strengthened conservative forces across parts of Latin America.

events.description: A cruise ship-turned-luxury floating hotel that has been idle for about 14 years rolled onto its side while docked.

events.title: Norway’s Waerenskjold wins fastest Tour de France stage in history
events.description: At an average speed of 50.91kph, Soren Waerenskjold's sprint was the fastest road stage in the 113 editions of the Tour.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: Shipwrecks of Shackleton and Scott recreated in 3D digital form after deep sea expedition
events.description: Canadian scientists visit remains of polar exploration vessels in ‘golden era for shipwreck investigating’ Moments after devouring the final glimmers of light, the seafloor offered nothing but darkness and silt. Then the bow appeared. More than 1,000ft (305 metres) below the surface of the Labrador Sea, off the coast of Canada, the skeleton of the final ship used by the famed polar explorer Ernest Shackleton appeared in its silty grave. Continue reading...

events.title: Greater gliders not as great at gliding as thought – but still look like magic carpets, experts say
events.description: While new study challenges assumptions about how far they travel, witnessing them in action still takes your breath away, scientists say Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast They may look like a fluffy flying carpet, but greater gliders are not as great at gliding as previously thought, Australian researchers have discovered. The first study to measure the aerial ability of Australia’s largest gliding marsupial has found they soar across an average distance of 19 metres – far shorter than the 100 metres suggested in an 85-year-old study. Continue reading...

events.title: Albanese’s AI blueprint sparks calls for datacentre moratorium until new regulations in place
events.description: Prime minister’s plan will create energy regulations for datacentres in Australia Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Environmental and community groups have called for a pause on datacentre development until new regulations are in place after Anthony Albanese promised “greater clarity and speed” over their approval. In his landmark speech on artificial intelligence at the University of Sydney on Wednesday, the prime minister said Australia would create a legal obligation for large-scale datacentres to underwrite new power supply, pay their full share of grid connection, and add as much energy to the grid as they take out of it. Continue reading...

events.title: Hong Kong booksellers arrested for allegedly selling seditious books
events.description: Hong Kong was once known for its freedom of publication, but political changes have created a challenging environment for independent bookstores.

events.title: China's economy grows 4.3% in Q2, slowest since late 2022
events.description: Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.

events.title: Greetings from Paris, where an art installation transformed the Pont Neuf into a rocky cave
events.description: The oldest bridge in Paris was draped in fabric last month to appear like a mountain cave, in an installation by the artist JR. Unlike a real cave, though, it didn't provide respite from a brutal heat wave.

events.title: American AI is expensive. Some startups are turning to cheap Chinese models
events.description: AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.

events.title: Retired vice admiral on whether the U.S. can control the Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Can the U.S. control the Strait of Hormuz? NPR's A Martínez asks retired Vice Admiral Kevin Donegan, who commanded the Navy's Fifth Fleet and the Combined Maritime Forces in the Middle East.

events.title: France's parliament passes assisted dying law
events.description: The lower house of France's parliament adopted bill for assisted dying. But the the government bypassed the right-wing dominated Senate and the law will instead go to the highest constitutional court for final approval.

events.title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes appeal to AfD voters
events.description: Germany's chancellor has insisted that his coalition has "regained its footing" — despite dismal poll numbers. Friedrich Merz also cautioned against voting for the far-right AFD party.

events.title: Kenya: Court rejects Rastafari bid for religious freedom marijuana exemption
events.description: Kenya's small Rastafarian community was seeking legal permission to smoke marijuana under the constitutional right to freedom of religion. The judge found they did not show it to be a necessary part of their worship.

events.title: Germany news: Coalition has 'found its footing,' Merz says
events.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said recent reforms show his coalition is making progress. Meanwhile, arrests were made after police carry out drug raids in western Germany and the Netherlands. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany news: Coalition has 'found its footing,' Merz says

events.title: Why investors are suddenly fleeing Indonesia
events.description: Indonesia, one of Asia's biggest economies, risks losing its emerging-market status as President Prabowo Subianto's spending plans rattle investors. Will the man who promised to deliver 8% growth rein in his ambitions?

events.title: India news: Landmark trade deal with UK takes effect
events.description: The free trade agreement allows zero-duty market access for nearly 99% of India's exports to the UK. Meanwhile, US senators have proposed a new bill to impose 100% tariffs on India for buying oil from Russia.
events.ticker_label: 📈 India news: Landmark trade deal with UK takes effect

events.title: Why is Zelenskyy reshuffling Ukraine's government?
events.description: Ukraine's Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has resigned, prompting a Cabinet reshuffle led by President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. What comes next?

events.title: Border controls scrapped between Spain and Gibraltar
events.description: As the end of border controls took effect, the old chain-link fence between Spain and Gibraltar was taken down. This historic decision allows for greater freedom of movement for people and goods.

events.title: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military tried to overthrow the Erdogan government. The consequences are still shaping Turkey today
events.description: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military attempted to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The consequences of the failed coup continue to shape Turkey to this day. DW looks back.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military tried to overthrow the Erdogan government. The consequences are still shaping Turkey today

events.title: Fast food nation? French chicken chain row gets political
events.description: On this particular television channel we do love covering the French and their love affair with fine food. But truth be told, eating habits here vary wildly be it by necessity, choice or habit. Don't say it too loudly but France boasts Europe’s highest number of McDonalds per person. So here we go with the feud between neighboring Paris suburbs over chicken drumsticks.

events.title: 🔴 France’s lower house adopts final text of landmark right-to-die law
events.description: No description available.

events.title: 🇦🇲🇷🇺Armenia - Russia: Armenian apricots caught in a geopolitical battle
events.description: In Armenia’s Ararat valley, apricots are getting swept into the centre of a geopolitical battle between Russia and the West. Angered at Yerevan's pivot away from Moscow, the Kremlin imposed bans and restrictions on the imports of a wide range of Armenian goods, including fruits, vegetables and flowers, ahead of parliamentary elections last month.

events.title: Norwegian Soren Waerenskjold wins fastest ever Tour de France stage
events.description: Norway's ​Soren Waerenskjold on Wednesday claimed ​his first stage victory, beating Belgian Jasper Philipsen in a ⁠mass sprint at the end of the ​161km 11th stage from Vichy to Nevers. At an average speed of 50.91kph, it was the fastest stage in the race's history.

events.title: Gus the T-rex breaks dinosaur fossil sale record at auction
events.description: A Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton has become the most expensive dinosaur fossil sold at auction after an unidentified buyer paid more than $50m (£37.3m) for it.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Gus the T-rex breaks dinosaur fossil sale record at auction

events.title: UK working to ban goods and services exports to and imports from Israeli settlements
events.description: The UK government is working on a ban on imports and exports to illegal Israeli settlements, the trade minister confirmed on Wednesday morning.

events.title: Gibraltar ushers in new era as British territory's border fence with Spain removed
events.description: Spain and Gibraltar are celebrating the removal of a border checkpoint following a historic treaty between the EU and the UK.

events.title: British man jailed with wife in Iran given additional sentence
events.description: A British man jailed in Iran along with his wife has been handed an additional two-year prison sentence, according to the couple's family.

events.title: Former Hungarian foreign minister resigns from parliament for job with Chinese automaker
events.description: Hungary's former foreign minister, Péter Szijjártó, has resigned from parliament to join Chinese electric automaker BYD

events.title: Starmer says his political journey is over at his last question session as UK leader
events.description: Keir Starmer has answered questions from lawmakers in the House of Commons for the last time before leaving the prime minister's office next week

events.title: The new malaria vaccine helps in Africa but faces dosing test
events.description: A recently introduced malaria vaccine is helping reduce severe illness in children across Africa

events.title: Skills for an uncertain future: How youth can navigate a changing job market
events.description: Choosing a path for the future has never been easy, but for young people today, such rapid technological change as artificial intelligence (AI) is making it increasingly difficult to predict which skills will remain relevant in the years ahead.

events.title: Florida school principals face DUI charges after police traffic stops just hours apart
events.description: Body camera video shows two Clermont, Florida principals arrested on DUI charges in separate traffic stops involving the same white Jeep SUV.

events.title: Slain American mother Jamey Carney remembered as 'ray of sunshine' at Ireland funeral
events.description: Jamey Carney's livestreamed funeral in Killarney celebrated her life as a "ray of sunshine" while the Jordanian suspect remains detained abroad.

events.title: Weightlifter accused of collecting $100K in disability while showing off in the gym online
events.description: Raymond Phillips allegedly posted weightlifting videos on social media while collecting over $100,000 in Social Security disability benefits.

events.title: Police say Canadian woman slapped teen over Trump and ICE clothing before ICE detained her
events.description: Surveillance video captured Kaitlyn Tracey allegedly slapping a teen over Trump and ICE pants on the Point Pleasant Beach boardwalk before her arrest.

events.title: Trump AG pick Todd Blanche faces Senate test and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Bank robbery suspect allegedly steals kitten, asks employee to hold it before demanding cash
events.description: A Maryland man allegedly stole a 3-month-old kitten from Pet Supplies Plus and handed her to a bank employee before attempting a cash robbery.

events.title: Who is Andy Burnham? The Trump critic set to become the UK's next prime minister
events.description: Andy Burnham secured 322 MP nominations on day one of the leadership process, but critics say he faces less scrutiny than any recent U.K. prime minister.

events.title: Trump officials unveil private sector blueprint for life after USAID
events.description: Ambassador Mike Waltz says the Trump administration is replacing traditional foreign aid with private investment and trade to create jobs, cut dependency.

events.title: FBI snares an American heir indicted for allegedly bankrolling anti-cop, pro-Hamas communist revolution
events.description: James Cox Chambers Jr., estranged heir to the $27 billion Cox empire, was detained in Spain for alleged money laundering and Hamas support.
